title: Food item (curated database)
description: One curated food entry. All user-facing text is a localized object keyed by language code ('de' required, others optional). Numbers are per 100 g edible portion unless stated otherwise. Tiers are deliberately coarse (1-3): this is a decision aid, not a lab report.

id.description: Stable slug, never changes once published. E.g. 'rote-linsen'.

category.description: Primary shelf category (German slugs as canonical keys; labels are localized in ui strings).

micros.description: Only micronutrients this food meaningfully contributes, as slugs: e.g. 'eisen', 'b12', 'jod', 'omega3', 'calcium', 'folat', 'zink', 'vitamin-c', 'vitamin-d'.

typical_portion.description: A realistic everyday portion, so the UI can translate per-100g values into per-portion values.

cost_tier.description: 1 = budget staple, 2 = normal, 3 = pricey. Judged per satiating portion in a German supermarket, not per kg.

convenience_tier.description: 1 = ready to eat / minutes, 2 = simple cooking, 3 = needs planning or skill (soaking, longer prep).

eco.description: Environmental footprint, coarse. Source: Poore & Nemecek 2018 aggregates or comparable; document the source per entry if it deviates.

co2_tier.description: 1 = low (most plants), 2 = medium (dairy, eggs, poultry, farmed fish), 3 = high (red meat, cheese, air-freighted goods).

co2_kg_per_kg.description: Optional exact value for the detail view.

tags.description: Dietary-pattern and filter tags as slugs: 'vegan', 'vegetarisch', 'glutenfrei', 'laktosefrei', 'proteinquelle', 'ballaststoffquelle', 'vorratstauglich', 'saisonal-sommer', ...

blurb.description: The curated heart of each entry: 2-4 sentences in the site's voice. Practical, concrete, no moral score. What it does well, what to watch, one everyday use.

swaps.description: IDs of foods this one can stand in for or be swapped with (drives Stage 1 'easy wins').

localized.description: Localized string: 'de' is canonical and required; add 'en' (and later others) as translations land. The UI falls back to 'de'.
